key_suppliers: Brooklyn Navy Yard Cogeneration Partners L.P.
supply_chain.dependencies: Dependence on interstate natural gas pipelines for gas supply; reliance on NYISO wholesale electricity markets for power purchases; single liquefied natural gas storage facility at Astoria property
risks: Supply chain disruptions and shortages of materials, equipment, labor; long lead times for replacement parts (transformers, microprocessors, semiconductors); global supply chain disruptions impacting electric transmission and distribution operations; geopolitical conflicts causing supply chain disruptions; extreme cold weather negatively impacting interstate natural gas system
Reliance on third-party contractors for gas and steam main welds; discovery of non-conforming welds from contractor misconduct; supply chain disruptions could impact operations

regulatory_risks: Compliance with gas and steam main weld quality regulations
EPA Section 111 Rule regulating GHG emissions from gas-fired combustion turbines
FERC authorization requirements for short-term borrowings and transactions
Customer billing system cost capitalization denial by NYSPSC
Net utility plant reconciliation requirements
Regulatory restrictions on dividends and intercompany transactions
Environmental regulations including Superfund liability for hazardous substances (asbestos, PCBs, coal tar)
NYSPSC focused operations audit on financial accounting for income taxes
Revenue decoupling mechanism and weather normalization clause adjustments
Environmental liability under Federal Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation and Liability Act (Superfund)
NYSPSC annual evaluation of customer privacy protections and critical energy infrastructure protection
Environmental regulations including CLCPA requiring 70% renewable electricity by 2030 and zero emissions by 2040
NYSDEC regulation imposing emissions limit on sulfur hexafluoride equipment starting 2030
Water quality permits under NYSDEC regulations
NYSPSC authority to revoke or modify operating certificates based on repeated violations
Environmental site investigation and remediation cost recovery
New York State Climate Leadership and Community Protection Act compliance
Lead-jacketed cable investigations by EPA, NYSDEC, and NYSDPS
Clean Air Act Title V operating permits and New Source Review regulations
NYSPSC rate plan approvals and regulatory oversight
NJBPU regulatory oversight for O&R New Jersey operations
Earnings sharing thresholds and regulatory asset recovery limitations
Late payment charge and uncollectible accounts recovery limitations
Property tax assessment changes and reconciliation limits
New York State Climate Change Superfund Act requiring responsible parties to pay $75 billion over 25 years
Rate plan provisions limiting rates charged to customers
Recurring independent third-party audits on regulations and standards
NYSPSC investigation of non-conforming welds and contractor misconduct
FERC regulation of transmission and wholesale sales with authority to impose substantial penalties
Cybersecurity regulations by FERC, TSA, CISA, and NYSPSC
NYSPSC authority to prohibit cost recovery deemed imprudently incurred
Regulatory treatment of pension and postretirement benefit costs
Extensive regulation by NYSPSC with authority to impose substantial penalties for violations
Rate plan provisions limiting recovery of certain costs (property taxes, municipal infrastructure support)
New York City Climate Mobilization Act requiring GHG emissions reduction from large buildings
Ring-fencing requirements separating utilities from parent company
Potential NYSPSC ordered adjustments to regulatory assets
Toxic Substances Control Act regulations on PCB-containing equipment
NYSDEC Peaker Rule limiting nitrous oxides emissions during ozone season
